rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ALAN DONES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Motion For Attorneys' Fees After Denial Of Petitioners' Petition Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1281.2
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 1, RESPONDENTS CALVIN GRIGSBY AND FISCAL FUNDING CO., INC.'s Motion For Attorneys' Fees After Denial Of Petitioners' Petition Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1281.2 Respondents Fiscal Funding Company, Inc. and Calvin Grisby's motion for attorneys' fees related to Petitioners Alan Dones and John Guillory's petition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2 is granted in the amount of $43,852.00. Respondents seek attorneys' fees for successfully defending Petitioners' motion to enjoin an ongoing arbitration between Petitioners and Respondent Fiscal Funding Company, Inc. On February 6, 2019, Petitioners initiated a separate action by filing a petition to enjoin arbitration proceedings, and thereafter filed a motion to grant petition to enjoin arbitration proceedings. The Court denied that motion by order filed March 29, 2019, finding that Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2 does not authorize court intervention to enjoin an ongoing arbitration. The general rule is that "fees under [Civil Code] section 1717 are awarded to the party who prevailed on the contract overall, not to a party who prevailed only at an interim procedural step." (DisputeSuite.com, LLC v. Scoreinc.com (2017) 2 Cal.5th 968, 977; accord, City of West Hollywood v. Khiagi (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 739, 753.) In DisputeSuite.com, the California Supreme Court discussed with approval several cases in which "a party has won a procedural victory, ending the contract litigation in one court but the litigation has continued or could continue in a different forum." (Id. at 975-77.) Those cases stand for the principle that "[a] party does not become the prevailing party under the statute merely by obtaining a forum for resolution of the contractual dispute or by moving it from one forum to another." (Id. at 976.) However, the Court distinguished Turner v. Schultz (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 974. Although that case is controlling here, Petitioners fail to mention it. Turner affirmed a fee award to defendants who, like Respondents, "obtained dismissal of the plaintiff's San Francisco action seeking to enjoin arbitration of a contract dispute between the parties that had been ordered . . . in an action filed by the same plaintiff." (Id. at 979.) Here, exactly as in Turner, "'The fees at issue were incurred in connection with an independent complaint for declaratory and injunctive relief . . . . [T]he only issue before the court-whether the arbitration should be allowed to proceed-was resolved in [Respondents'] favor in this discrete legal proceeding . . . . Under the circumstances, it appears that [Respondents'] entitlement to attorney fees in this legal action is independent of the outcome of the arbitration of the merits of the underlying dispute . . . .'" (Id. at 980, quoting Turner, 175 Cal.App.4th at 983.) The same conclusion follows here. Petitioners' petition to enjoin arbitration proceedings was an independent action that put only the question of arbitration at issue, and not the substance of the contractual dispute that is the subject of the pending arbitration. (See also Otay River Constructors v. San Diego Expressway (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 796, 807-08 [holding that where an action is brought solely to compel arbitration of contractual disputes between the parties, a party who succeeds in obtaining an order denying the petition to compel arbitration is a prevailing party in the action on the contract even though the merits of the parties' underlying contractual disputes have not yet been resolved, because the case involved "the final resolution of a discrete legal proceeding"].) The Court awards $43,852.00, which it finds to be a reasonable amount in the light of the issues presented by the underlying motion and the instant motion. SEE PART 2 OF 2 FOR ENTIRE TENTATIVE RULING. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ENCARNITA ALONSO VS. GURBAKSH CHAHAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Encarnita Alonso To Lift Stay Of Action Issued On Arbitration Grounds
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ENCARNITA ALONSO's Motion Of Plaintiff Encarnita Alonso To Lift Stay Of Action Issued On Arbitration Grounds Plaintiff Encarnita Alonso's motion to lift stay of action issued on arbitration grounds is granted. The court orders the stay lifted and the matter restored to calendar because of the arbitration's termination due to Defendants' failure to pay arbitration fees. (Dhillon Decl., Exh.9). (See Cinel v. Christopher (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 759, 769 n.11 ["the arbitration proceedings had not taken place due to failure to pay fees, and thus the trial court regained full jurisdiction, in the language of the statute, at 'such earlier time as the court' specified."].) Defendant Gurbaksh Cahal's late-filed objection to the motion lacks merit. Cahal asserts that the motion was not properly served on Defendants, but the proof of service reflects that it was served on Defendants by overnight mail on May 1, 2019 at the address they provided to JAMS in connection with the arbitration, as well as on counsel to Defendant Gravity4, Inc. in California and Delaware. In any event, Defendant admits that he learned of the motion, and the Court has considered his objection. The objection does not dispute Plaintiff's showing that the arbitration was terminated due to Defendants' failure to pay the arbitration fees. Further, Cahal appears to be under the misimpression that Plaintiff is seeking a default judgment, rather than simply to lift the stay. Defendants' assertions regarding the merits of the underlying action can be raised in the course of the litigation, once the stay is lifted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALI R. SHAKOORI VS. SHABAN A SHAKOORI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Answer And Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTs SHABAN SHAKOORI and JULIA SHAKOORI's Motion To Amend Answer And Cross-Complaint Defendants and Cross-Complainants Shaban A. Shakoori and Julia Shakoori's motion to amend answer and cross-complaint is granted. California's strong policy favoring liberal amendment of pleadings supports the motion. Plaintiff Ali R. Shakoori has failed to show that the proposed amendments are barred by the sham pleading doctrine. "Critically, the sham pleading doctrine 'cannot be mechanically applied.' [Citation.] It 'is not intended to prevent honest complainants from correcting erroneous allegations or prevent the correction of ambiguous facts.'" (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 678, 690.) Further, the doctrine "was not intended to preclude plaintiffs from providing additional and noncontradictory allegations." (Leasequip, Inc. v. Dapeer (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 394, 405 n.6; see also Zakk v. Diesel (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 431 [new allegations regarding contract claims in third amended complaint "merely clarifies the basis for [plaintiff's] claims for relief" and sham pleading doctrine therefore did not apply].) Plaintiff may raise any challenge he has to the timeliness of the proposed new causes of action in the cross-complaint by demurrer or motion for summary judgment/adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Anna Gatti'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery And Request For Sanctions As To Loop Ai Labs Inc. And Gianmauro Calafiore
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 2, Plaintiff Anna Gatti'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery And Request For Sanctions As To Loop Ai Labs Inc. And Gianmauro Calafiore (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is granted as follows: The discovery in the previous federal court action has not been shown to be identical to the discovery requests propounded in this state court action, except as to documentary and electronic materials produced which may be equally applicable in both proceedings and which would impose an unnecessary burden to produce in physical or electronic form a second time. Moreover, the knowledge and information available to the responding parties may differ from that which they previous held, such that new matter may necessarily be disclosed in response to the state court discovery requests. Finally, responding parties have not cited binding authority that discovery in a similar prior federal which was dismissed on procedural grounds necessarily supplants any obligation to comply with discovery requests in a later state court action. The plaintiff's meet and confer efforts were adequate under the circumstances of this case and the unitary nature of the main discovery issue to be resolved. Accordingly, the responding parties shall within 30 days after the date of order hereon provide further responses as to the "Disputed Responses" listed for each and all of the eight sets of discovery requests identified on page 2, lines 7-26 of the notice of this motion and shall produce documents and other matter responsive to the each and all of the Disputed Responses of the Requests for Production of Documents either by individual identification of documents previously produced or physical production of documents not previously produced. Documents, individuals and institutions referred to in response to interrogatories shall be identified individually by unique identifier as to documents or name and address as to persons or institutions. Objections except as to privilege are overruled. Documents withheld on grounds of privilege shall be listed in a privilege log. Sanctions are denied. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CRUZ VS. BAY AREA GREEN CROSS DISPENSARY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Compliance With Plaintiffs Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DAVID CRUZ'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Plaintiffs Subpoenas Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court will hear argument as to whether this motion to enforce any remaining subpoenas should be denied because (1) plaintiff's action arises under Labor Code Section 302.5 which does not require proof that the defendant had actually committed improper activity, but only that the employee plaintiff reasonably believed that such activity had occurred and suffered retaliation for properly reporting it; (2) defendants hold heightened privacy protection rights in their confidential financial information; (3) plaintiff has not shown that he cannot get the information by less intrusive means. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. On May 17, 2019, Defendants rejected the arbitrator's award and filed a request for trial. As a result, no judgment will be entered on the award or on Plaintiff's premature memorandum of costs. (See Cal. R. Ct. 3.827(a)(1) ["The clerk must enter the award as a judgment immediately upon the expiration of 60 days after the award is filed if no party has, during that period, served and filed . . . A request for trial as provided in these rules."].) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DARRELL RINERSON VS. DAVID DYER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Darrell Rinerson'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Sustaining Demurrer To First Amended Without Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 10, Plaintiff Darrell Rinerson'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Sustaining Demurrer To First Amended Without Leave To Amend Plaintiff Darrell Rinerson's motion for reconsideration of the Court's April 10, 2019 order sustaining Defendant David Dyer's demurrer to the first amended complaint without leave to amend is denied. While Plaintiff seeks leave to file a second amended complaint based on certain facts that occurred after he filed his opposition to Defendant's demurrer, the new allegations he seeks to add cannot overcome the dispositive defects identified by the Court in its prior order. His attempt to reargue the Court's prior order is improper, and he fails to address controlling authority cited in that order. He also fails to show that the other arguments he asserts present new or different facts, circumstances or law that he could not with reasonable diligence have presented at the first hearing. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1008(a); New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212.) Plaintiff asserts that on April 4, 2019, he "re-obtained complete ownership of 25% shares of ELI stock." (Mot. at 2.) However, Plaintiff admits that he sold his interest in ELI in April 2017, and he filed the original complaint in this action on August 9, 2018. As the Court indicated in its order, with exceptions not pertinent here, "California law 'generally requires a plaintiff in a shareholder's derivative suit to maintain continuous stock ownership throughout the pendency of the litigation." (Order at 2, quoting Grosset v. Wenaas (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1100, 1119.) Thus, Plaintiff's "now current ownership of ELI shares" (Mot. at 2) does not warrant reconsideration. "Plaintiff admittedly did not maintain continuous ownership in the company throughout the period this litigation has been pending, and therefore lacks standing to assert derivative claims on its behalf." (Order at 3.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER Z. SILVA VS. CINDY ANN PARDINI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CINDI PARDINI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. By order dated March 21, 2019, the Court sustained Defendant's demurrer to the complaint with 20 days' leave to amend, and took Defendant's motion to strike the complaint off calendar as moot. The Court granted Plaintiff leave to plead a claim for slander and/or libel that (1) occurred within one year of the filing of the complaint on September 28, 2018, and (2) does not involve privileged conduct such as a police report or statements made in a judicial proceeding. The Court also directed Plaintiff to remove the allegations regarding time-barred or privileged conduct. On April 11, 2019, Plaintiff filed a 6-page [first amended] "complaint for damages" that fails to comply with the Court's prior order and largely suffers from the same defects as his original complaint. Although Plaintiff reportedly called Defendant's counsel on May 21 indicating his intent to provide an "amended filing," he has not opposed the motion or timely sought leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER Z. SILVA VS. CINDY ANN PARDINI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CINDI PARDINI's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted without leave to amend. No opposition filed. By order dated March 21, 2019, the Court sustained Defendant's demurrer to the complaint with 20 days' leave to amend, and took Defendant's motion to strike the complaint off calendar as moot. The Court granted Plaintiff leave to plead a claim for slander and/or libel that (1) occurred within one year of the filing of the complaint on September 28, 2018, and (2) does not involve privileged conduct such as a police report or statements made in a judicial proceeding. The Court also directed Plaintiff to remove the allegations regarding time-barred or privileged conduct. On April 11, 2019, Plaintiff filed a 6-page [first amended] "complaint for damages" that fails to comply with the Court's prior order and largely suffers from the same defects as his original complaint. Although Plaintiff reportedly called Defendant's counsel on May 21 indicating his intent to provide an "amended filing," he has not opposed the motion or timely sought leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY ** MIGUEL, INC. VS. BRUCE MADDOX ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stipulated Order To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF MIGUEL, INC.'s Motion For Stipulated Order To Transfer Venue Granted pursuant to the parties' stipulation. The court will execute the proposed order but add that plaintiff is responsible for all transfer fees. The case is transferred to Alameda County. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIRA RIVERA VS. HINES GS PROPERTIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DRYWALL CO.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant California Drywall Co.'s demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The allegations of the first amended complaint disclose that plaintiff was on inquiry notice with regard to her claim at the latest by October 2015. (First Amended Complaint, 33, 39, and 40.) Counsel Kleczek, who represents other plaintiffs with claims similar to plaintiff Rivera's, explains that these allegations only pertained to the other plaintiffs and they have no relevance to plaintiff Rivera's claim. (Kleczek Dec.) The court finds that counsel's drafting error provides a plausible explanation for allowing amendment and the sham pleading doctrine does not apply. (See Smyth v. Berman (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th183, 195.) Plaintiff therefore has leave to amend to allege facts regarding when plaintiff Rivera became injured and/or the mode and manner when she discovered her injury to render her claim timely. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 3, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT HINES GS PROPERTIES, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Hines GS Properties, Inc.'s motion to strike is granted with 20 days leave to amend.
